Floor commit 8cfe17b violated its own .gitattributes eol=lf policy: dispositions.jsonl and anchors.jsonl committed as CRLF on Windows host despite policy. CT-2 substrate review at COW-1 v4 endorsement validated JSON+schema but not EOL bytes; sha256 match (4ce30ce0…36d5da CRLF) gave false confidence on byte-shape. Caught by ET-1.W verify-live pre-append. Normalized to LF in this commit: 9 prior lines re-normalized + 4 new lines appended LF-native. Floor sha256 superseded by LF-canonical hash. Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #26.

LF normalization pipeline in ET-1.W paired-commit build silently fail-opened on /tmp path mismatch: Python content-guard hit FileNotFoundError instead of aborting; bash tr/heredoc path did not yield LF; commit 5535db6 stored CRLF blob despite intent. Caught post-commit by ET-1.W verify-live (CR-lines=12 vs expected 0), recovered via git reset --soft 8cfe17b (unpushed, no remote impact). Redo built file as true LF via Python open(...,newline='') with cwd-relative temp path and hard gate (CR==0 AND lines==13 AND content-guard 1-9==floor). Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #27. D-error-26 statement now true in this commit (LF normalization actually occurred); floor sha256 4ce30ce0...36d5da superseded by LF-canonical hash captured post-commit.
evidence: Python build with newline='' + cwd-relative temp
hard gate: CR==0 AND lines==13 AND content-guard PASS

statement: D-error-26 over-claimed anchors.jsonl as CRLF. Verify-live shows only dispositions.jsonl was committed CRLF at floor 8cfe17b; anchors.jsonl was committed LF (CR-bytes=0, sha256 3b9822fd...652e5c, eol=lf). The anchors-CRLF assertion was an unverified extrapolation from the dispositions.jsonl finding and from ET-1.W flags that repeated it; anchors EOL was never independently checked until ET-1.W pre-push verify-live. Consequence: no anchors normalization was required and none was performed; the ledger was already fully LF-compliant after b4a1f09 (dispositions.jsonl 13 lines LF + anchors.jsonl 3 lines LF). D-error-26's dispositions.jsonl-CRLF half remains accurate; its anchors.jsonl half is corrected by this entry. Logged as Doctrine #97 case study #28.
